topic.name: Defense and Health Policy
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative acts related to defense appropriations, military health care, and community service programs.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for procurement for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, Air Force, and Department of Defense (DOD) for aircraft, missiles, weapons and combat vehicles.
Prohibits Army aircraft funds from being obligated or expended for the Airborne Common Sensor until certain certifications are met.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles.
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from procuring more than two brigade sets of early-infantry brigade combat team increment one equipment.
Document 4: Establishes in the Department of Defense an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Directs the Secretary of Defense to require DOD policies for victim assistance and family advocacy programs.

topic.name: Tariff and Trade Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule and other trade regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on 1,3-propanediaminium...
suspend temporarily the duty on certain sardines in oil...
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to modify the article description of polyisocyanate...
extend temporarily the suspension of duty on such article.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain sardines in oil...
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on man-made shells...
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain wooden wall plates.

topic.name: Government Budget and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to budgetary processes, appropriations, and accountability measures within the federal government.

Document 1: establish in its Committee on Appropriations a Subcommittee on Intelligence
public disclosure of certain intelligence funding information
Document 2: Religious Freedom Peace Tax Fund for the deposit of income, gift, and estate taxes
allocated annually to any appropriation not for a military purpose
Document 3: limitations on the use of public funds or property
assist, deter, or promote union organizing
Document 4: use of arbitration to resolve a controversy that arises under a contract
private civil action for a violation of any rights or protections
Document 5: modify the provision governing the composition of the membership of the Science Advisory Board
appointment of up to three Board members based on a recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture
Document 6: authorize the President to propose the cancellation (line item veto) of any dollar amount of discretionary budget authority
requirements for the President's transmittal to Congress of a special message
Document 7: require the concurrent resolution on the budget for FY2012 to include a detailed benchmark plan to reduce discretionary spending
makes it out of order in both chambers to consider any budget resolution for any fiscal year through 2020 if it is in violation of this Act

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States pertaining to temporary suspensions of duties on various products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary duty suspension on methyl hydroxyethyl cellulose products.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily suspend the duty on certain programmable slow cookers.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on pigments based on titanium dioxide.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on light emitting diode (LED) display modules (LDM).
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on Calcium chloride phosphate activated by manganese and/or antimony for use in the production of fluorescent lamps.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on certain capers in immediate containers each holding more than 3.4 kilograms.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain woven mesh fabric.

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing oversight, accountability, and transparency in various sectors, including small business contracting, electoral integrity, financial institutions, tax compliance, and data security.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to include under penalties for misrepresentation the willful misrepresentation of status as a small business for the purpose of obtaining, retaining, or completing a federal contract.
Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to issue rules defining an advisory opinion for such purposes.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful for anyone before or during a federal election to knowingly communicate, or attempt to communicate, false election-related information about that election.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to review and, if appropriate, amend the federal sentencing guidelines and policy statements applicable to persons convicted of any offense under this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity, insurance company, or swaps entity from serving simultaneously as an officer, director, employee, or other institution-affiliated party of any insured depository institution.
Document 4: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to tax shelter activities to establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions.
Increases penalties for promoting abusive tax shelters and for aiding and abetting the understatement of tax liability.
Document 5: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to review credit ratings and methodologies employed by each nationally recognized statistical rating organization (NRSRO).
Requires each NRSRO to establish and enforce governance procedures to manage conflicts of interest in accordance with SEC rules.
Document 6: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish specified security policies and procedures to treat and protect such information.
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses (pretexting).
Document 7: Expresses the sense of Congress with respect to the establishment by foreign governments of requirements that companies under their jurisdiction publicly disclose any payments made to a government relating to the commercial development of oil, natural gas, and minerals.

Requires the Secretary of the Interior to disclose to the public any payment relating to the commercial development of oil, natural gas, and minerals on federal land made by any person to the government.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments to Medicare and Medicaid aimed at improving healthcare coverage and access for various medical services and professionals.

Document 1: Amends part B of title XVIII (Medicare) to include federally recommended vaccines under Medicare part B.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to include podiatrists as physicians in order to cover their services under the Medicaid program.
Document 3: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) to require qualified dietary management in nursing facilities.
Document 4: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to prescribe special rules for Medicare coverage of individuals receiving a heart transplant.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to direct payments for diabetes prevention services for prediabetic Medicare beneficiaries.
Document 6: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to eliminate the Independent Payment Advisory Board for Medicare.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to provide for reimbursement of clinical social worker services.

topic.name: Healthcare Policy and Improvement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing healthcare quality, access, and affordability within various healthcare systems, particularly Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to prescribe standards for appointment and practice as a physician within the Veterans Health Administration.
Prohibits a new appointment without approval of the director of the Veterans Integrated Services Network.
Document 2: Creates a cross reference to provisions in the Medicare, Medicaid, and SCHIP Balanced Budget Refinement Act of 1999.
Requires the Secretary to approve distinct part rehabilitation units in certain long-term care hospitals.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a program requiring a manufacturer to provide a rebate.
Requires that payment for both a drug or biological functionally equivalent to another drug or biological and that other drug or biological to be the same as for the least costly of them.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make incentive payments to rural health clinic employees or contractors for satisfactory reporting of data on clinic quality measures.
Expresses the sense of the Senate concerning the adequacy of network-based health plans.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to request the National Association of Insurance Commissioners to conduct reviews every five years of the national and state-specific markets for long-term care insurance policies.
Sets forth requirements related to the use of the proposed model disclosure form for marketing LTC insurance policies.
Document 6: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to terminate limitations on general Medicaid funding for U.S. territories.
Establish a formula for Medicaid disproportionate share hospital payments to the territories.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to require the issuer of the plan to treat such individual as enrolled as of December 23, 2013.
Counts coverage provided under a qualified plan for January and February 2014 under this Act as coverage under such a plan.

topic.name: Public Health and Environmental Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing health risks and environmental hazards through regulation of drugs, pollutants, and safety standards.

Document 1: Preventing Antibiotic Resistance Act of 2013 - requires demonstration of no harm to human health due to antimicrobial resistance.
Defines 'medically important antimicrobial' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals.
Document 2: Vessel Discharge Evaluation and Review Act - directs evaluation of vessel discharges and establishes national discharge standards.
Preempts related state and local laws regarding a discharge incidental to the normal operation of a vessel.
Document 3: Oil Pollution Prevention and Response Act of 2009 - requires measures to reduce the risk of oil spills.
Establishes requirements for the promulgation of safety standards for towing vessels.
Document 4: Pathogen Reduction and Testing Reform Act of 2014 - revises the definition of 'adulterated' to include microbial pathogens.
Requires USDA to establish sampling protocols and testing procedures for adulterated products.
Document 5: Boutique Fuel Reduction Act of 2007 - amends the Clean Air Act regarding fuel distribution and control waivers.
Requires notice and opportunity for comment before the Administrator removes a fuel from the list.
Document 6: Clean Rivers Act of 2011 - directs review of pipeline regulations crossing rivers.
Requires establishment of a publicly available database of pipeline water crossings.
Document 7: Safe Access in Fire Emergencies Act of 2009 - requires safety standards for window bars in residences.
Directs CPSC to establish a National Electronic Injury Surveillance System code for window bars.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to energy efficiency, renewable energy, and environmental protection in the context of U.S. energy policy.

Document 1: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution, transitioning to a clean energy economy
establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions
Document 2: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf
Requires approval by law of an adjacent state for oil or gas leasing
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make state grants for extension, education, applied research, and development activities at institutions of higher education
provide funds for basic research on next generation technology
Document 4: prohibit any person from exporting any natural gas from the United States to a foreign country without first having secured an order of the Secretary of Energy
requires the Secretary to issue an environmental impact statement (EIS)
Document 5: directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to provide for a farm's base acreage adjustment for covered commodities
requires producers to comply with specified conservation requirements in order to receive payments
Document 6: require each retail electric supplier to submit to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) a quantity of federal renewable electricity credits
prohibits standards for any year from being lower than the standard for 2030
Document 7: authorizes the head of each executive agency to establish projects under which an agency head may offer to enter into cost-effective power purchase agreements
requires the Secretary to establish a federal facility energy efficiency and renewable energy projects fund program

topic.name: Immigration and Child Welfare Reform
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative reforms related to immigration policies, child welfare, and the protection of vulnerable populations, particularly focusing on children and families.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Child Protection and Family Violence Prevention Act
prohibit any foster care placement over which an Indian tribe has exclusive jurisdiction from being ordered until the tribal social services agency completes an investigation
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include a 'permanent partner' within the scope of such Act
Defines a 'permanent partner' as an individual 18 or older who is in a committed, intimate relationship
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise provisions regarding the detention and removal of aliens ordered removed
Extends the removal (and detention) period beyond 90 days
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish a nonimmigrant W-1 visa for an alien whose status in the United States is on a conditional basis
has maintained a continuous physical presence in the United States during the five-year period
Document 5: Amends title II of the Social Security Act with respect to any case in which the Social Security account number in the wage records of an employee does not match relevant records
informing the employer that a copy of such notice is being forwarded to assist the Secretary of Homeland Security
Document 6: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of State to negotiate agreements for the repatriation of children
Subjects all children who do not make a claim of asylum or fear of persecution to initial screening and expedited removal
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to check against all available terrorist watchlists those alien smugglers and smuggled individuals
Provides extraterritorial jurisdiction over such offenses

topic.name: Coast Guard and Military Appropriations
topic.description: The documents discuss various acts related to the authorization and appropriation of funds for the Coast Guard and military construction projects.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2013-FY2014 appropriations for the Coast Guard
Designates amounts from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund to carry out specified provisions of the Oil Pollution Act of 1990.
Document 2: Revises provisions concerning the leadership of the Coast Guard
Requires the Commandant to promote maritime safety according to specified priorities.
Document 3: Terminates all authorizations contained in Titles XXI through XXVI
Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects.
Document 4: Appropriates funds for FY2009 for the Department of Defense
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of funds appropriated in this title.
Document 5: Requires executive agencies to ensure that inherently governmental functions are performed by federal employees
Prohibits an agency from conducting a public-private competition for any function that such plan identifies as experiencing a gap in its federal employee workforce.
Document 6: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to establish and carry out a program to provide equity investment financing
Establishes a separate Treasury fund for such grants.
Document 7: This bill amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2013
The Small Business Administration (SBA) must implement a specified plan for improving the quality of data reported on bundled or consolidated contracts.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative measures related to foreign policy, emphasizing sanctions and diplomatic actions towards specific countries, including Cuba, Israel, Syria, and Iran.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba.
Authorizes the President to impose export controls with respect to Cuba.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should take specified actions to assist in Israel's defense.
Directs the President to report to Congress on the status of Israel's qualitative military edge.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of the Senate that the United States condemns the violence and human rights violations perpetrated against the Syrian people.
States that the purposes of assistance under this Act are to support democratic, inclusive transition in Syria.
Document 4: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should continue to support diplomatic efforts in the International Atomic Energy Agency.
It should be U.S. policy to encourage foreign governments to direct state-owned and private entities to cease all investment in Iran's energy sector.
Document 6: States that it shall be U.S. policy to undertake measures to deny the government of Cuba financial resources.
Directs the President to impose specified export sanctions if investments are made that contribute to Cuba's ability to develop petroleum resources.

topic.name: Tax Reforms and Benefits
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various tax reforms aimed at providing benefits to individuals, families, and businesses through amendments to the Internal Revenue Code.

Document 1: exempt such benefits paid to eligible beneficiaries from applicable employment and unemployment taxes
allow self-employed individuals a tax deduction for the health insurance costs of their eligible beneficiaries
Document 2: establish new estate tax rates
allow a $1 million estate tax exclusion
Document 3: allow an exclusion from gross income for amounts received on account of a claim of unlawful discrimination
an exemption from the alternative minimum tax for any tax benefit resulting from the income averaging
Document 4: limit the amount of direct and indirect government assistance by the governments of Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands to rum producers
impose limitations on the allocation of rum excise taxes
Document 5: establish tax-exempt ABLE accounts to assist an individual with a disability
requires amounts in ABLE accounts to be disregarded in determining eligibility for Medicaid
Document 6: repeal the inclusion of unemployment compensation in gross income
allow tax-free distributions from retirement plans for unemployed individuals
Document 7: increases the limit on the amount of the tax credit for small employer pension plan startup costs
allows a three-year $500 business-related tax credit for small employers

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Public Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at addressing public policy issues across different sectors, including transportation, social services, and veterans' affairs.

Document 1: Amends the Death on the High Seas Act
provisions concerning commercial aviation accidents
Document 2: Transit-Oriented Development Infrastructure Financing Act
eligible for TIFIA funding
Document 3: Amends civil service law
authorize the use of clinical social workers
Document 4: New Jersey Betting and Equal Treatment Act
exempt a lottery, sweepstakes, or other betting
Document 5: Amends title IV part D of the Social Security Act
mandatory revocation of passports for child support arrears
Document 6: Separation of Powers Act of 2015
prohibits the use of funds appropriated
Document 7: Honoring Our Veterans By Exercising The Right To Vote On Veterans Day Act
Moves Veterans Day from November 11

topic.name: Federal Policy Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at addressing specific social, health, and environmental issues through coordinated programs and research efforts.

Document 1: Amends the 21st Century Nanotechnology Research and Development Act to expand the scope of the National Nanotechnology Program
Requires issuing guidance to agencies that describes a strategy for transitioning research into commercial products
Document 2: Establishes within the Department of State the Office for Orphan Policy Diplomacy and Development
Directs the President to establish the Policy Coordinating Committee which shall monitor and support international efforts in family preservation
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to establish the Interagency Prostate Cancer Coordination and Education Task Force
Directs the Secretary to establish and carry out a program to coordinate and intensify prostate cancer research
Document 4: Directs the Department of Justice's (DOJ's) Bureau of Justice Assistance (BJA) to award grants to state and local law enforcement
It also limits the use of grants under this bill for conferences that use more than $20,000 in DOJ funds
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a Civil War Sesquicentennial Commemoration Commission
Directs the National Endowment for the Humanities to award grants for appropriate activities relating to the Civil War sesquicentennial
Document 6: Directs the President to implement a National Water Research and Development Initiative to improve the federal government's role
Requires the Plan to: establish priorities for federal water research
Document 7: Amends the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to establish a coastal climate change adaptation planning and response program
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to coastal states to develop such plans

topic.name: Biofuels and Clean Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the development, funding, and promotion of biofuels and clean energy technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes a Biofuels Investment Trust Fund
assist farmers, producers, biorefiners, researchers, universities, and other persons or entities involved in the research, development, and deployment, or production of biofuels
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to enter into one or more contracts for the procurement of liquid transportation fuel
meets requirements of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 relating to the procurement and acquisition of alternative fuels
Document 3: develop and publish near-, medium-, and long-term goals for the deployment of clean energy technologies
establish or promote specified energy generation, transmission, and use
Document 4: require a reduction in the income and excise tax credits for alcohol used for fuel
ensure that at least 50% of model year automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured for sale in the United States are dual fueled
Document 5: establish a federal energy efficiency resource standard for retail electricity and natural gas distributors
set such standards at levels reflecting the maximum achievable level of cost-effective energy efficiency potential
Document 6: extend through 2015 the income and excise tax credits for alcohol used as fuel
the cellulosic biofuel producer tax credit
Document 7: establish a Clean Energy Technology Manufacturing and Export Assistance Fund
promote policies that will reduce production costs and encourage innovation, investment, and productivity in the clean energy technology sector

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on protecting consumers in financial transactions and lending practices.

Document 1: revises the process for exercising the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to make purchases of troubled assets
prohibits the Secretary from exercising any authority to make any such purchases unless a joint resolution approving the plan is enacted into law.
Document 2: amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (EFTA) to declare that a remotely created check may only be issued by a person specifically designated in writing by the consumer
prohibits issuance of any payment order in response to a consumer's exercise of federal consumer financial rights.
Document 3: prohibits a creditor from taking an application for a reverse mortgage unless the creditor has provided the applicant with a specified written cautionary statement
requires a counseling agency to provide the same kind of written checklist to a consumer that seeks counseling before requesting a loan application for a reverse mortgage.
Document 5: prohibit the annual percentage rate applicable to any extension of credit on a credit card account under an open end consumer credit plan from exceeding 20%.
Document 7: amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Board to exempt from certain escrow or impound requirements a loan secured by a first lien on a consumer's principal dwelling
requires the SEC to conduct cost-benefit analyses of certain new or amended generally accepted accounting principles.

topic.name: Water Rights and Land Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to water rights, land management, and tribal recognition, focusing on the use and protection of water resources across various states.

Document 1: Arizona Water Settlements Act
Southern Arizona Water Rights Settlement effective as of the date the Secretary of the Interior publishes
Document 2: Idaho Wilderness Water Resources Protection Act
special use authorization to the owners of a water storage, transport, or diversion facility
Document 3: Hunter and Farmer Protection Act
amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to revise standards for determining baiting
Document 4: Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to Miami-Dade County
federally owned land comprising the Subtropical Horticulture Research Station
Document 5: Action Plan for Public Lands and Education Act of 2011
grants of land to states for support of common schools
Document 6: Lumbee Recognition Act
extend federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
Document 7: Montana Mineral Conveyance Act
requires the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Great Northern Properties

topic.name: Education Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving education through funding, support services, and community engagement.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to add bullying and gang prevention to the violence and drug abuse prevention activities
Provides that... such program should facilitate healthy, physically active lifestyles.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to states
Integrate financial literacy and entrepreneurship education into their public middle and secondary schools.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Education (ED) to award National Classified School Employee Awards
ED shall choose an awardee each year, out of nominations received from each state.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make competitive grants to local governments
Requires such programs to include neighborhoods with characteristics such as proximity to educational institutions.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD), each fiscal year, to provide an increase in the rates of educational assistance
Educational assistance payable to certain members of the Selected Reserve.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to award grants to institutions of higher education
Increasing fire safety awareness among enrolled students.
Document 7: Authorizes the Department of Education (ED) to award grants to states and Indian tribes to waive tuition and fees at community colleges
Appropriates $1.4 billion in FY2016 and $79.7 billion over FY2016-FY2025 to make grants under this community college program.

topic.name: Housing Finance and Assistance
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to housing finance, mortgage regulation, and assistance programs aimed at improving access to housing and financial support for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Directs the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) to prescribe classifications for mortgages having various degrees of credit risk
requires the Director to establish standards for specified categories of underwriting criteria for each classification of mortgages depending on degree of credit risk.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act with respect to Federal Housing Administration (FHA) housing loan insurance for manufactured homes
Directs the Secretary of HUD to establish underwriting criteria for loans and credit in connection with a manufactured home.
Document 4: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to subject a small public housing agency (PHA) to the same requirements as a PHA
Requires HUD to evaluate the management of a small PHA's voucher program solely on the basis of its lease-up rate or the budget utilization rate.
Document 6: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act to make additional FY2017-FY2021 appropriations available for emergency relief grants to address the unmet needs of homeless populations

The bill provides funding to HUD to expand the Healthcare and Housing (H2) Systems Integration Initiative by assisting states and localities in coordinating policies among Medicaid programs, behavioral health providers, housing providers, and finance support services for homeless persons.

topic.name: Designations of Public Facilities
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative actions related to the naming and designation of various public facilities and buildings in the United States and abroad.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4000 Leap Road in Hilliard, Ohio, as the 'Master Sergeant Shawn T. Hannon, Master Sergeant Jeffrey J. Rieck and Veterans Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the U.S. Embassy headquarters building in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, as the 'Mickey Leland United States Embassy Building.'
Document 3: Designates the federal building currently known as Federal Office Building 8, located at 200 C Street SW in the District of Columbia, as the 'Thomas P. O'Neill, Jr. Federal Building.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 115 4th Avenue Southwest in Ardmore, Oklahoma, as the 'Specialist Micheal E. Phillips Post Office.'
Document 7: This bill designates as the Alex Diekmann Peak the 9,765-foot peak located 2.2 miles west-northwest of Finger Mountain.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Employment
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving benefits and employment opportunities for veterans, including financial compensation, training programs, and health services.

Document 1: military retired pay should not be reduced
receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation
Document 2: five-year pilot project to test the feasibility and advisability of the use of educational assistance
training costs associated with the purchase of a franchise enterprise
Document 3: revises the definition of 'qualified veteran' for purposes of the work opportunity tax credit
provides documentation relating to eligibility for and use of such credit
Document 4: repeal the currently required 30-day minimum period of internment
adds diabetes (type 2) to the listed diseases
Document 5: provide certain medical and dental services to members of the Selected Reserve
funding for such services through the Defense Health Program
Document 6: prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting debts owed to the United States by certain veterans
termination of collection is in the best interest of the United States
Document 7: conduct a five-year pilot project to test the feasibility and advisability of the use of educational assistance
training and entity approval requirements

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at regulating specific industries and practices, including environmental protection, food safety, social security, firearms, drug sentencing, and animal welfare.

Document 1: Prohibits any person from offering billfish (except swordfish) or billfish products for sale
Subjects a person to a maximum civil penalty of $100,000 for each violation.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Requires the periodic testing of such foods transferred along a chain of distribution.
Document 3: Prohibit a state, local government, or any officer, employee, or contractor of a state or local government, from displaying to the general public on the Internet all or any portion of any Social Security account number
Establishes a fine of up to $5,000 a day on any state or local government that has a policy or practice of substantial noncompliance.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the civil penalties for violations of firearms law
Requires fines to be based upon the nature and severity of the violation.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to direct the court to impose a sentence for specified controlled substance offenses without regard to any statutory minimum sentence
Authorizes a court that imposed a sentence for a crack cocaine possession or trafficking offense to impose a reduced sentence.
Document 6: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to promulgate requirements for the exercise of dogs at facilities owned or operated by a dealer.
Document 7: Amends the Controlled Substances Act and the Controlled Substances Import and Export Act
Directs the United States Sentencing Commission to review and amend its sentencing guidelines.

topic.name: Water Rights and Environmental Management
topic.description: The documents discuss legislation related to water rights, environmental management, and protection of natural resources in various regions.

Document 1: Navajo-Hopi Little Colorado River Water Rights Settlement Act of 2012
Requires all water rights of the Navajo Nation and the Hopi Tribe to be held in trust
Document 2: Bureau of Reclamation Transparency Act
directs the Secretary of the Interior to submit to Congress, make publicly available, and biennially update an Asset Management Report
Document 3: Gulf of the Farallones and Cordell Bank National Marine Sanctuaries Boundary Modification and Protection Act
U.S. policy to protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
Document 4: Lake Tahoe Restoration Act of 2015
The bill adds additional requirements for the management of the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit
Document 5: Grazing Improvement Act of 2012
Amends the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 to double from 10 to 20 years the period of a term for grazing permits
Document 6: amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act
authorize the Bureau of Reclamation to participate in projects to benefit eligible communities through projects to capture and treat wastewater
Document 7: Virginia Ridge and Valley Act of 2007
Designates certain lands in the Jefferson National Forest as wilderness

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax relief and incentives for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow the medical expenses of dependents
to be paid from a health savings account.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual and business taxpayers a tax credit
for 25% of their qualified hurricane and tornado mitigation property expenditures.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend until February 15 the annual deadline
for reporting certain investment income to the Internal Revenue Service.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the limit on the deductibility of losses
from the sale or exchange of capital assets for individual taxpayers.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a 100% tax deduction for qualified manufacturing activities income.
Defines 'qualified manufacturing activities income' as the excess of domestic manufacturing gross receipts.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers an exclusion from gross income
for overtime pay.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an offset against the alternative minimum tax
of alternative motor vehicle tax credit amounts.
